mnemonics: Enumerate Your Zoo Items Carefully (for E-Z-I-C): Enumerate, Zip, Iter, Next
keyPoints: enumerate(iterable) returns index and value pairs, useful for tracking position in a loop
zip(*iterables) combines multiple iterables element-wise, creating tuples from each position
iter() and next() provide manual control over iteration, allowing custom stopping conditions
commonMistakes: Forgetting that zip() truncates to the shortest iterable, potentially losing data
Modifying the iterable while iterating over it, causing unexpected behavior or errors
Using next() without handling StopIteration exception, which will crash the program
